data.title: merge-to-main: local main version drift -> plugin.json conflict on retry after interrupted version_bump
data.description:
## Incident Overview
When merge-to-main.sh completes version_bump but fails before push (e.g. SIGURG/timeout), local main retains the bumped version. On the next merge-to-main invocation, the merge phase conflicts because the worktree (synced to origin/main) has the old version and local main has the bumped version.

## Expected Behavior
merge-to-main.sh should detect that local main is ahead of origin/main on plugin.json version and reset or reconcile before attempting the merge.

## Actual Behavior
merge-to-main --resume retries the merge phase but hits an unresolvable plugin.json conflict. The squash-rebase recovery also fails. The only fix is manually running git -C MAIN_REPO reset --hard origin/main.

## Observed
baff-7163 session: local main had version 1.12.26, origin/main had 1.12.25. Merge conflict blocked merge-to-main until manually reset.

## Escalation Policy: Escalate when blocked
